message:
chore(beads): sync issues.jsonl with closed pinning beads + new tracking entries

Re-syncs .beads/issues.jsonl to reflect the latest beads state across the
parallel pinning-test sweep landed in this branch (and earlier in the day).
Net effect: ~52 lines added / ~10 modified, all of them updated_at /
closed_at / close_reason field bumps as agents marked their assigned
pinning beads complete.

Notable transitions absorbed:

- Many "Pin fcp-core …" beads moved to closed with their landing-commit
  SHAs in close_reason (e.g. ZoneId equality, ConnectorVersion comparison,
  ProvenanceId Display, RequestId Display, CredentialId Display, SecretFormat
  serde tags, EnforcementCheckId Display, CapabilityConstraints predicates,
  ConnectorId case sensitivity, OperationId Display, hostname validator,
  SignedManifest signature roundtrip, scope intersection algebra,
  RequestId monotonic generation, duration Display, relative-time parser).
- A handful of new in_progress beads were added for HashAlgorithm serde
  tag matrix and HealthState transitions (the work landed in this branch).
- One previously-closed CRDT merge ordering bead got its close_reason
  retitled to "shipped at 74bee1fc1" with no behavior change.

Pure metadata / tracking sync — no source code, no test code, no
configuration changes. Equivalent to running `bd sync` (or the br
flush-only equivalent) and committing the resulting JSONL drift.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
